text.title: Workflow Design Phase Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how workflow-design-phase produces a valid design.md artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application: JWT refresh feature
example.code:
--- artefact_id: design-jwt-refresh owner: Ruslan Faion <ruslan@faion.net> version: 1.0.0 last_reviewed: 2026-05-23 feature: jwt-refresh status: Accepted spec_ref: .aidocs/features/in-progress/jwt-refresh/spec.md ---
## Codebase Survey
- `apps/auth/views/login.py` — already uses SimpleJWT; pattern to extend. - `apps/auth/tests/test_login.py` — pytest-django style; reuse fixtures. - `apps/core/middleware.py` — JWT cookie handling; respect existing flow.
## Architectural Decisions
### AD-2: HS256 + 15-min access TTL + 7-day refresh TTL + rotation on use
- Chosen: HS256, rotate refresh on every successful use. - Rejected: RS256 (overkill for monolith key-management); no rotation (replay window too wide). - Rationale: HS256 keeps key management simple; rotation closes theft window. - Satisfies: FR-3, NFR-2. - Trade-off: HS256 secret shared across processes; mitigated by single-instance deploy.
## File Table
| Action | Path | Scope | |--------|------|-------| | CREATE | apps/auth/views/refresh.py | POST /auth/refresh view + rotation logic | | MODIFY | apps/auth/urls.py | Wire new URL | | CREATE | apps/auth/tests/test_refresh.py | Tests for AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-1.3 |
## FR Coverage
| FR | AD | |----|-----| | FR-3 | AD-2 | | NFR-2 | AD-2 |
## Testing Strategy
- AD-2 covered by integration tests against the live SimpleJWT pipeline.
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to a concrete spec; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
